title: execute_notebook_edit cell_type for replace is silently allowed to switch types without resetting fields
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Functional Correctness / Tell-Dont-Ask

## Location
src/tools/file/notebook.rs:183-186 (replace branch cell_type assignment)

## Violation
```rust
cells[index]["source"] = source_to_line_array(new_source);
if let Some(ct) = cell_type {
    cells[index]["cell_type"] = json!(ct);
}
```

If the cell was previously a "code" cell with `outputs: [...]` and `execution_count: 5`, and the replace passes `cell_type: "markdown"`, the cell's stale `outputs` and `execution_count` fields remain. Markdown cells should NOT have those fields per nbformat spec — Jupyter clients reading the file may reject or display the cell oddly. Inverse case: replacing a markdown cell with a code cell does NOT add the required `outputs: []` and `execution_count: null` fields.

Worse, the cell_type value is accepted verbatim — there's no allowlist. `cell_type: "raw"` (a valid nbformat type) and `cell_type: "garbage"` (invalid) are both accepted; the latter produces a corrupt notebook.

## Mandated refactor
1. Validate cell_type against ["code", "markdown", "raw"] at extraction time.
2. When the cell_type changes on a replace, run the same field-normalization that insert uses (line 218-226):
   - For new type "code": ensure outputs=[], execution_count=null exist.
   - For new type "markdown" or "raw": remove outputs and execution_count if present.
3. Centralize the "build a well-formed cell of type T" logic in one helper used by both insert and replace-with-cell_type paths (DRY — currently duplicated).

## Filed from QA review dispatch
